_skill.name: lap_annotation
_skill.description: Per-label telemetry characteristics for the 10 candidate parent labels (EA, MSP, MSR, RM, PS, O, OD, MD + circuit) that the lap-to-segment excerpter can attach to one circuit section. Each label declares its telemetry signature (trajectory_offset, time_difference_to_expert slope, brake overlap, etc.) and the global_rules block prescribes the per-section detection procedure. Source of truth for "does this section qualify as MSP / RM / EA / ... at all?"
when_to_use: Lap excerpter assigning one parent label per circuit section.
Building the system prompt for the lap-flow planner/synthesizer.
Evaluator checks for parent-label justification against telemetry.

brands_hatch.name: Brands Hatch
brands_hatch.applies_when: lap recorded at Brands Hatch (Static_track resolves to brands_hatch)
brands_hatch.characteristics: Circuit parent label — mandatory on every section of a Brands Hatch lap. No telemetry check needed beyond confirming the track ID.

silverstone.name: Silverstone
silverstone.applies_when: lap recorded at Silverstone (Static_track resolves to silverstone)
silverstone.characteristics: Circuit parent label — mandatory on every section of a Silverstone lap. No telemetry check needed beyond confirming the track ID.

EA.name: Expert Adherence
EA.characteristics:
Player tracks the expert through the section.
  - `trajectory_offset` stays within ±0.5 m of zero for the whole range.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` slope is ≈ 0 (gap neither grows nor
    shrinks) — confirm with `compute_slope`.
  - Speed delta at sampled ilocs is small.
Mutually exclusive with MSP and RM.

MSP.name: Mistake (Practice)
MSP.characteristics:
Technical driving error causing time loss or instability, NOT caused by interaction with a close opponent.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` rises continuously (gap grows) — confirm
    with `compute_slope` (positive) and `find_extremum` (max - min > 0).
  - 'time_difference_to_expert' slope rises at the end of the corner exit, this
    may indicate a mistake.
  - `trajectory_offset` exceeds the expert envelope: a spike outward
    (ran wide) or inward (cut tight) that does NOT recover within the
    section.
  - `find_nearest_opponent(start, end)` returns NO close primary
    candidate, OR the candidate's outcome flags do NOT explain
    the time loss. A close opponent + adverse outcome ⇒ MSR.
Mutually exclusive with EA, RM, MSR. The detailed-annotation flow refines MSP sub-ranges later — attach MSP at the section level when the section is dominated by a technical error.

MSR.name: Mistake (Racing)
MSR.characteristics:
Time / position loss caused by an interaction with a close opponent inside the section.
  - Call `find_nearest_opponent(start, end)`. Require a primary
    candidate with `min_distance_m` small OR
    `side_by_side_iloc_count > 0`.
  - Adverse interaction outcome: `passed_by_player: false`
    (failed attempt — MSR1) OR `got_passed_by_opponent: true`
    (defense broken — MSR2).
  - `time_difference_to_expert` typically grows across the
    interaction window.
Mutually exclusive with EA, RM, MSP. No close opponent ⇒ MSP, not MSR.

RM.name: Recovery & Merge
RM.characteristics:
Player recovering from a prior mistake inside this section.
  - `time_difference_to_expert` slope is negative across the range
    (gap shrinks, never grows) — confirm with `compute_slope`.
  - `trajectory_offset` trends back toward zero from a non-zero start.
Often appears in the section immediately following an MSP. Do NOT attach when the player started at offset ≈ 0 — that's EA, not RM. Mutually exclusive with EA and MSP.

PS.name: Pit Stop
PS.characteristics:
Player entering or sitting in the pit lane. Merging back out from the pit toward the expert line is RM, not PS.
  - `distance_to_expert_line` magnitude persistently large
    (≥ ~5 m) AND NOT trending toward zero (a decreasing offset
    ⇒ merging out ⇒ RM, not PS).
Picking which named catalog section to attach is a sub-label decision — see `sub_label_annotation`. Compatible with the circuit label only; do NOT combine with EA / MSP / MSR / RM / O / OD.

O.name: Successful Overtake
O.characteristics:
Player gained a position on a specific opponent inside the section. Multi-car positional data (`Car_{1..60}_pos_{x,y,z}`) is the gate.
  - Call `find_nearest_opponent(start, end)`. Require a primary
    candidate with `passed_by_player: true` — signed longitudinal
    gap flips from positive (opp ahead at entry) to negative
    (opp behind at exit). Empty `candidates` OR no
    `passed_by_player` flag set ⇒ do NOT attach O.
  - Player-trace evidence to ALSO hold: late-brake signature
    past the expert reference, sustained outside / inside line
    deviation NOT explained by MSP / RM, or speed > expert on
    equal-or-less throttle (draft).
A close opponent + failed attack is MSR at the section level — sub-labelled `MSR1` in the detailed flow. A successful defense is OD, not O.

OD.name: Successful Defense
OD.characteristics:
Player held position against a real, close opponent inside the section.
  - Call `find_nearest_opponent(start, end)`. Require a primary
    candidate with `got_passed_by_opponent: false` AND a close
    engagement (`side_by_side_iloc_count > 0` OR
    `min_distance_m` ≤ ~10 m). No close candidate ⇒ not OD.
  - Player-trace evidence to ALSO hold: brake initiation
    earlier than expert and/or trajectory tighter than expert
    (inside cover), or a throttle lift on a straight with no
    brake onset (defensive lift). Time delta need not be flat
    — it may grow briefly during the defense as long as the
    position holds.
A defense that failed (opponent got past) is MSR at the section level — sub-labelled `MSR2` in the detailed flow.

MD.name: Missing data
MD.characteristics:
Section contains telemetry gaps, dropouts, or corrupted values.
  - A signal that should be dynamic flatlines (e.g. speed = 0 mid-lap).
  - Physically impossible values (negative speed, gear out of range).
  - NaN runs inside the section.
Trigger: a `find_extremum` returns an impossible min/max, or a rendered graph shows a flat plateau where motion should exist. Compatible with every other label.

global_rules:
Per-section detection procedure:
1. **Anchor the circuit + section.** The circuit label comes from the lap
   context (always attached). The splitter assigned a `circuit_section_id`;
   call `locate_circuit_section(start, end)` once to confirm the rough
   range still overlaps that section's `normalized_position_range`. When
   the response has `is_ambiguous: false`, attach `best_match.label_id`.
   When `is_ambiguous: true`, enumerate `top_matches` and read each
   entry's `description` — the catalog carries any per-section
   disambiguation tell. Use `peek_graph` or out-of-range
   `query_telemetry` if the disambiguating evidence sits just before
   or after the section.

2. **Render the two diagnostic graphs.** One `render_graph` each over the
   section's iloc range:
     - `trajectory_offset` — discriminates EA vs. MSP vs. RM vs. PS.
     - `time_difference_to_expert` — confirms gap direction (flat ⇒ EA,
       rising ⇒ MSP, falling ⇒ RM).
   These two cover EA / MSP / RM / PS detection. Add a third only when a
   specific candidate (O / MD) needs it.

3. **Score each candidate label against its `characteristics` block.**
   Use `query_telemetry` for quantitative confirmation:
     - `compute_slope` on `time_difference_to_expert` → sign and magnitude
       of the gap trend.
     - `find_extremum` on `trajectory_offset` → peak deviation from zero.
     - `find_first_match` on `Physics_brake` / `Physics_gas` → onset ilocs
       when O needs late-brake evidence.
   For O / OD / MSR candidates also call
   `find_nearest_opponent(start, end)`:
     - O requires `passed_by_player: true` on the primary candidate.
     - OD requires `got_passed_by_opponent: false` AND a close
       engagement (`side_by_side_iloc_count > 0` OR `min_distance_m`
       ≤ ~10 m).
     - MSR requires a close primary candidate AND an adverse outcome
       (`passed_by_player: false` for MSR1, or
       `got_passed_by_opponent: true` for MSR2).
   No close candidate ⇒ rule out all three (O / OD / MSR); if the
   section is still off-line / slow it falls to MSP.
   A section may attach at most ONE of {EA, MSP, MSR, RM} (mutually
   exclusive) plus optionally PS / O / OD / MD. O / OD / MSR are
   opponent-aware labels and only one of them can apply (the same
   range cannot be both a successful overtake and a defense, etc.).
   Attaching none of {EA, MSP, MSR, RM} is valid when telemetry is
   too noisy to commit.

4. **Refine the boundary for parent-label fit.** The characteristics
   blocks assert their signature across the WHOLE range. If the picked
   main label fits only part of the rough window (offset clean for the
   first half then spikes, gap rises only late, etc.), or the signature
   clearly continues past the rough end, call `revise_segment_range`
   to shrink or extend so ONE signature holds uniformly. The working
   envelope is the lap range, so outward extension is allowed. Re-render
   the diagnostic graphs on the new range before scoring again.

5. **Optionally pick one ST1–ST6** from the label catalog descriptions
   when the trajectory shape is unambiguous. Use `compute_expert_phases`
   to help: empty phases ⇒ straight (ST2), one arc ⇒ in/approach/exit
   corner (ST1/ST3/ST4), multi-arc with no flat gap ⇒ ST6. Skip the ST
   pick entirely when the shape is ambiguous.

6. **Submit** via `submit_lap_proposal` with
   `label_ids = [circuit, circuit_section_id, ...optional ST, ...optional main labels]`
   and a `reasoning` string citing the ilocs / slopes / offset peaks you
   observed.

Hard rules:
  - One proposal per agent run; the UI loops one section per click.
  - Never invent label IDs — only IDs from the eligible list are accepted.
  - EA + MSP, EA + MSR, EA + RM, MSP + MSR, MSP + RM, MSR + RM are
    all contradictions — pick at most one from {EA, MSP, MSR, RM}.
  - O + OD, O + MSR, OD + MSR are all contradictions — pick at most
    one opponent-aware label per range.
  - PS is incompatible with EA / MSP / RM / O / OD.
  - Empty `label_ids` `[]` is the valid "drop this section" signal.
  - Final iloc range must remain inside the lap and ≥ 3 ilocs.
